title: A Fragment.
content:
What are the falling rills, the pendant shades,
    The morning bowers, the evening colonnades,
    But soft recesses for th' uneasy mind
    To sigh unheard in, to the passing wind!
    So the struck deer, in some sequester'd part,
    Lies down to die (the arrow in his heart);
    There hid in shades, and wasting day by day,
    Inly he bleeds, and pants his soul away.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alexander Pope, titled "A Fragment.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
